title: Hard edit checks are opt-in via bind::oc:constraint-type and required-type
description: By default, constraints in OC are soft (warning-only) — the user can save the form with a constraint violation and a warning is logged. To make a constraint hard-rejecting, add bind__oc_constraint_type='hard'. Same mechanism for hard-required fields via bind__oc_required_type='hard'. Hard checks block save; soft checks allow save with a warning.
rationale: OC4's default soft-constraint behavior is appropriate for most clinical-trial data entry, where coordinators need flexibility to capture imperfect real-world data and document the deviation. Hard checks should be reserved for data-integrity-critical fields (eligibility verdicts, randomization-blocking criteria, regulatory-required fields) where saving an invalid value would cause downstream harm. The opt-in mechanism keeps the default safe and the override explicit.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-6 during Phase B.1a. Kind: advisory — the prose in prompts.py documents the OC opt-in mechanism rather than asserting a policy. A separate structured policy convention (e.g. 'default to soft; flag required=yes rows without required-type set') could be proposed in B.1+ but would be a new convention, not a translation of OC-6. Reclassification from structured to advisory was made during B.0 review (see B0_inventory.md System 1 review note on row 1.8).
